text:
The repo exists. The Git clone URLs are:

| Protocol | URL |
|---|---|
| **HTTPS** | `https://github.com/tommy-hylt/team-ai-3.git` |
| **SSH** | `git@github.com:tommy-hylt/team-ai-3.git` |
| **GitHub CLI** | `gh repo clone tommy-hylt/team-ai-3` |

The repo is at **`https://github.com/tommy-hylt/team-ai-3`** (Public). Your local repo currently has no remote configured — would you like me to add one of these as the `origin` remote?

Done. Here's what was created/updated:

**New skill:** `.claude/skills/cleanup-prepare/SKILL.md`

The skill instructs Claude to:
1. **Summarize** — tasks completed, methodology, key learnings
2. **Update existing skills** — patch SKILL.md files with corrections or new notes
3. **Create new skills** — if a reusable workflow was discovered
4. **Compact MEMORY.md** — write session learnings in, prune stale content, keep under 150 lines

**MEMORY.md updates:**
- Added `cleanup-prepare` to the Existing Skills list
- Added the GitHub repo URL (`tommy-hylt/team-ai-3`) and note that no remote is configured locally
- Added two new Browser Adaptor notes about finding the GitHub username via `[data-login]` and why the API approach fails
